title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: R4SUB framework organises submission-readiness evidence four pillars: quality, traceability, risk, usability. Usability measures easy regulatory reviewer understand navigate submission package — dimension six major regulatory authority profiles allocate 15 % 20 % total submission-readiness weight. r4subusability implements four indicators make pillar: Every assessment function returns validated R4SUB evidence table via r4subcore::as_evidence(), output immediately compatible scoring profiling layers.

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: four indicators driven shared configuration list. defaults reflect common regulatory requirements, can override element: Key parameters: label_min_chars / label_max_chars — acceptable label length window (default: 3–40 characters). required_origins — origins must derivation text ("Derived", "Assigned"). reviewer_guide_keywords — strings identify reviewer guide asset ("ADRG", "SDRG", "reviewers_guide", "reviewer_guide", "ARG"). weights — relative indicator weights summing 1.
code: cfg <- usability_config_default() str(cfg) #> List of 5 #>  $ label_min_chars        : int 3 #>  $ label_max_chars        : int 40 #>  $ required_origins       : chr [1:2] "Derived" "Assigned" #>  $ reviewer_guide_keywords: chr [1:5] "ADRG" "SDRG" "reviewers_guide" "reviewer_guide" ... #>  $ weights                :List of 4 #>   ..$ label_quality      : num 0.3 #>   ..$ define_completeness: num 0.35 #>   ..$ annotation_coverage: num 0.25 #>   ..$ reviewer_guide     : num 0.1 cfg$weights #> $label_quality #> [1] 0.3 #>  #> $define_completeness #> [1] 0.35 #>  #> $annotation_coverage #> [1] 0.25 #>  #> $reviewer_guide #> [1] 0.1

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: assessment functions require run context r4subcore. single context object typically created per pipeline execution:
code: ctx <- r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY01") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112449-wl4dieex"

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: assess_label_quality() checks every variable label : Presence — missing empty label fail severity = "high". Minimum length — label shorter label_min_chars warn. Maximum length — label longer label_max_chars warn. Otherwise — pass severity = "info" metric_value = 1.
code: meta_labels <- data.frame(   dataset  = c("ADSL",    "ADSL", "ADAE"),   variable = c("USUBJID", "AGE",  "AETERM"),   label    = c(     "Unique Subject Identifier",  # OK — 26 chars     "Age",                         # OK — 3 chars (at minimum)     ""                             # fail — empty label   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  ev_label <- assess_label_quality(meta_labels, ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 3 rows ev_label[, c("location", "result", "severity", "metric_value", "message")] #>       location result severity metric_value                message #> 1 ADSL.USUBJID   pass     info            1      USUBJID: label OK #> 2     ADSL.AGE   pass     info            1          AGE: label OK #> 3  ADAE.AETERM   fail     high            0 AETERM: label is empty

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: assess_define_completeness() verifies Define-XML metadata populated correctly. variables whose origin required_origins, non-empty derivation text mandatory. USUBJID AETERM come CRF — derivation required, pass. AGE "Derived" derivation text, also passes. variable origin = "Derived" derivation text fail:
code: meta_define <- data.frame(   dataset    = c("ADSL",    "ADSL",   "ADAE"),   variable   = c("USUBJID", "AGE",    "AETERM"),   label      = c(     "Unique Subject Identifier",     "Age",     "Adverse Event Term"   ),   origin     = c("CRF",     "Derived", "CRF"),   derivation = c(NA,        "Derived from BRTHDTC and RFSTDTC", NA),   codelist   = c(NA,        NA,        NA),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  ev_define <- assess_define_completeness(meta_define, ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 3 rows ev_define[, c("location", "result", "severity", "message")] #>       location result severity                          message #> 1 ADSL.USUBJID   pass     info USUBJID: origin 'CRF' documented #> 2     ADSL.AGE   pass     info       AGE: derivation documented #> 3  ADAE.AETERM   pass     info  AETERM: origin 'CRF' documented meta_bad <- data.frame(   dataset    = "ADSL",   variable   = "TRTEDT",   label      = "Treatment End Date",   origin     = "Derived",   derivation = NA_character_,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ev_bad <- assess_define_completeness(meta_bad, ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row ev_bad[, c("location", "result", "severity", "message")] #>      location result severity                                         message #> 1 ADSL.TRTEDT   fail     high TRTEDT: derivation missing for origin 'Derived'

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: assess_annotation_coverage() works dataset level. dataset computes proportion derived variables non-empty derivation text: >= 90 % → pass 70 – 89 % → warn < 70 % → fail ADSL 2 derived variables, 1 annotated → 50 % → fail. ADAE derived variables coverage applicable (na).
code: meta_annot <- data.frame(   dataset    = c("ADSL",   "ADSL",   "ADSL",   "ADAE",   "ADAE"),   variable   = c("AGE",    "SEX",    "TRTEDT", "AESTDTC","AEENDTC"),   origin     = c("Derived","CRF",    "Derived","CRF",    "CRF"),   derivation = c(     "Derived from BRTHDTC and RFSTDTC",     NA,                                   # CRF — not counted     NA,                                   # Derived but not documented     NA,                                   # CRF — not counted     NA                                    # CRF — not counted   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  ev_annot <- assess_annotation_coverage(meta_annot, ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 2 rows ev_annot[, c("asset_id", "result", "metric_value", "message")] #>   asset_id result metric_value                                        message #> 1     ADSL   fail          0.5 ADSL: 50% of derived variables annotated (1/2) #> 2     ADAE     na          1.0                     ADAE: no derived variables

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: assess_reviewer_guide() checks character vector asset names keyword matches reviewer_guide_keywords (case-insensitive substring match). returns single evidence row.
code: # Submission package includes an ADRG ev_guide_pass <- assess_reviewer_guide(   assets = c("ADRG", "define.xml", "datasets"),   ctx    = ctx ) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row ev_guide_pass[, c("result", "severity", "message")] #>   result severity                                             message #> 1   pass     info Reviewer guide asset detected in submission package # Submission package is missing a reviewer guide ev_guide_fail <- assess_reviewer_guide(   assets = c("define.xml", "datasets"),   ctx    = ctx ) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row ev_guide_fail[, c("result", "severity", "message")] #>   result severity                                                      message #> 1   fail     high No reviewer guide (ADRG/SDRG) detected in submission package

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: usability_indicators() runs four assessments single call returns one combined evidence table:
code: meta_full <- data.frame(   dataset    = c("ADSL",    "ADSL",    "ADAE"),   variable   = c("USUBJID", "AGE",     "AETERM"),   label      = c(     "Unique Subject Identifier",     "Age",     "Adverse Event Term"   ),   origin     = c("CRF",     "Derived", "CRF"),   derivation = c(NA, "Derived from BRTHDTC and RFSTDTC", NA),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  assets <- c("ADRG", "define.xml")  ev_all <- usability_indicators(meta_full, assets = assets, ctx = ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 3 rows #> ✔ Evidence table created: 3 rows #> ✔ Evidence table created: 2 rows #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row #> ✔ Bound 4 evidence tables: 9 total rows  # One row per variable per indicator, plus one row for the reviewer guide nrow(ev_all) #> [1] 9 table(ev_all$indicator_id, ev_all$result) #>         #>         na pass #>   U-001  0    3 #>   U-002  0    3 #>   U-003  1    1 #>   U-004  0    1

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: usability_summary() produces structured usability_result object per-indicator pass/fail/warn counts overall score. print method renders colour-coded console report: can also access underlying summary data frame programmatically:
code: res <- usability_summary(ev_all) print(res) #>  #> ── R4SUB Usability Assessment ── #>  #> Study: "STUDY01" | Variables assessed: 6 #>  #> ✓ Variable Label Quality: 100% pass (3/3) #> ✓ Define-XML Completeness: 100% pass (3/3) #> ✗ Annotation Coverage: 50% pass (1/2) #> ✓ Reviewer Guide Presence: 100% pass (1/1) #>  #> ℹ Overall usability score: 87.5% res$summary[, c("indicator_id", "indicator_name", "n_pass", "n_fail", "pct_pass")] #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   indicator_id indicator_name          n_pass n_fail pct_pass #>   <chr>        <chr>                    <int>  <int>    <dbl> #> 1 U-001        Variable Label Quality       3      0      1   #> 2 U-002        Define-XML Completeness      3      0      1   #> 3 U-003        Annotation Coverage          1      0      0.5 #> 4 U-004        Reviewer Guide Presence      1      0      1

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: Override specific defaults without replacing entire list:
code: cfg_strict <- usability_config_default() cfg_strict$label_min_chars <- 5L   # stricter minimum length cfg_strict$label_max_chars <- 30L  # tighter maximum  ev_strict <- assess_label_quality(meta_full, ctx, config = cfg_strict)

title: Usability Indicators with r4subusability
text: evidence table returned r4subusability function plugs directly rest R4SUB pipeline: Evidence can also persisted audit trails:
code: # Downstream — illustrative, requires r4subscore and r4subprofile score  <- r4subscore::compute_score(ev_all) report <- r4subprofile::render_profile(score, authority = "FDA") tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") r4subcore::export_evidence(ev_all, tmp, format = "rds") ev_reloaded <- r4subcore::import_evidence(tmp, format = "rds")

title: r4subusability — Usability Indicators for R4SUB
text: r4subusability usability pillar package R4SUB ecosystem. quantifies reviewer-experience indicators clinical regulatory submissions, assessing variable label quality, Define-XML completeness, dataset annotation coverage, reviewer guide presence. Evidence rows follow standard R4SUB schema r4subcore, making directly compatible r4subscore (SCI scoring) r4subprofile (authority-specific profiling).

title: r4subusability — Usability Indicators for R4SUB
text: Development version:
code: install.packages("r4subusability") pak::pak(c("R4SUB/r4subcore", "R4SUB/r4subusability"))

title: r4subusability — Usability Indicators for R4SUB
code: library(r4subusability)  meta <- data.frame(   dataset  = c("ADSL", "ADAE"),   variable = c("USUBJID", "AETERM"),   label    = c("Unique Subject Identifier", "Reported Term for the Adverse Event"),   origin   = c("Assigned", "CRF"),   type     = c("text", "text"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  results <- usability_indicators(meta, study_id = "STUDY-001") usability_summary(results)

title: r4subusability — Usability Indicators for R4SUB
text: MIT

title: Assess Dataset Annotation Coverage — assess_annotation_coverage
text: Checks proportion derived variables derivation text documented. Returns one evidence row per dataset summarising annotation coverage, indicator_id = "U-003".

title: Assess Dataset Annotation Coverage — assess_annotation_coverage
code: assess_annotation_coverage(metadata, ctx, config = NULL)

title: Assess Dataset Annotation Coverage — assess_annotation_coverage
text: metadata data frame columns dataset, variable, origin, derivation. ctx run context created r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(). config configuration list usability_config_default(). NULL default configuration used.

title: Assess Dataset Annotation Coverage — assess_annotation_coverage
text: validated evidence tibble (see r4subcore::as_evidence()).

title: Assess Dataset Annotation Coverage — assess_annotation_coverage
code: ctx <- r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY01") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112446-wl4dieex" meta <- data.frame(   dataset    = c("ADSL", "ADSL", "ADSL"),   variable   = c("AGE", "SEX", "RACE"),   origin     = c("Derived", "CRF", "Derived"),   derivation = c("Derived from BRTHDTC", NA, NA),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ev <- assess_annotation_coverage(meta, ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row ev$metric_value #> [1] 0.5

title: Assess Define-XML Completeness — assess_define_completeness
text: Evaluates whether datasets variables metadata required Define-XML fields populated (label, origin, derivation derived variables, codelist reference applicable). Results returned R4SUB evidence rows indicator_id = "U-002".

title: Assess Define-XML Completeness — assess_define_completeness
code: assess_define_completeness(metadata, ctx, config = NULL)

title: Assess Define-XML Completeness — assess_define_completeness
text: metadata data frame columns dataset, variable, label, origin (character), derivation (character, may NA), codelist (character, may NA). ctx run context created r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(). config configuration list usability_config_default(). NULL default configuration used.

title: Assess Define-XML Completeness — assess_define_completeness
text: validated evidence tibble (see r4subcore::as_evidence()).

title: Assess Define-XML Completeness — assess_define_completeness
code: ctx <- r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY01") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112447-ombeu7fd" meta <- data.frame(   dataset    = c("ADSL", "ADSL"),   variable   = c("USUBJID", "AGE"),   label      = c("Unique Subject Identifier", "Age"),   origin     = c("CRF", "Derived"),   derivation = c(NA, "Derived from BRTHDTC"),   codelist   = c(NA, NA),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ev <- assess_define_completeness(meta, ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 2 rows nrow(ev) #> [1] 2

title: Assess Variable Label Quality — assess_label_quality
text: Evaluates quality variable labels metadata data frame. variable checked label presence, length within recommended range, absence prohibited characters. Results returned R4SUB evidence rows indicator_id = "U-001".

title: Assess Variable Label Quality — assess_label_quality
code: assess_label_quality(metadata, ctx, config = NULL)

title: Assess Variable Label Quality — assess_label_quality
text: metadata data frame least columns dataset (character), variable (character), label (character). ctx run context created r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(). config configuration list usability_config_default(). NULL default configuration used.

title: Assess Variable Label Quality — assess_label_quality
text: validated evidence tibble (see r4subcore::as_evidence()).

title: Assess Variable Label Quality — assess_label_quality
code: ctx <- r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY01") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112447-78yvg5fi" meta <- data.frame(   dataset  = c("ADSL", "ADSL", "ADAE"),   variable = c("USUBJID", "AGE", "AETERM"),   label    = c("Unique Subject Identifier", "Age", ""),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ev <- assess_label_quality(meta, ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 3 rows nrow(ev) #> [1] 3

title: Assess Reviewer Guide Presence — assess_reviewer_guide
text: Checks whether reviewer guide document (Analysis Data Reviewer's Guide Study Data Reviewer's Guide) declared submission asset list. Returns evidence rows indicator_id = "U-004".

title: Assess Reviewer Guide Presence — assess_reviewer_guide
code: assess_reviewer_guide(assets, ctx, config = NULL)

title: Assess Reviewer Guide Presence — assess_reviewer_guide
text: assets character vector asset names file names present submission package (e.g. c("ADRG", "define.xml", "datasets")). ctx run context created r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(). config configuration list usability_config_default(). NULL default configuration used.

title: Assess Reviewer Guide Presence — assess_reviewer_guide
text: validated evidence tibble one row (see r4subcore::as_evidence()).

title: Assess Reviewer Guide Presence — assess_reviewer_guide
code: ctx <- r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY01") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112447-vj57qv4a" ev_present <- assess_reviewer_guide(c("ADRG", "define.xml"), ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row ev_missing <- assess_reviewer_guide(c("define.xml"), ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row ev_present$result #> [1] "pass" ev_missing$result #> [1] "fail"

text: Invisibly returns x. Called side effect printing summary usability indicator scores (pass/fail/warn counts overall proportion) console.

what: r4subusability: Usability Indicators for Clinical Submission Readiness — r4subusability-package
title: r4subusability: Usability Indicators for Clinical Submission Readiness — r4subusability-package
text: Quantifies reviewer-experience usability indicators clinical regulatory submissions. Assesses variable label quality, Define-XML (Extensible Markup Language) completeness, dataset annotation coverage, reviewer guide presence. Emits standardised R4SUB (R Regulatory Submission) evidence table rows via 'r4subcore' integration 'r4subscore' 'r4subprofile'.

title: r4subusability: Usability Indicators for Clinical Submission Readiness — r4subusability-package
text: Maintainer: Pawan Rama Mali prm@outlook.(ORCID) [copyright holder]

title: Default Usability Assessment Configuration — usability_config_default
text: Returns default configuration list used usability_indicators() individual assessment functions.

title: Default Usability Assessment Configuration — usability_config_default
code: usability_config_default()

title: Default Usability Assessment Configuration — usability_config_default
text: named list following elements: label_min_chars Minimum recommended label length (integer). label_max_chars Maximum recommended label length (integer). required_origins Character vector origins require derivation text (e.g. "Derived", "Assigned"). reviewer_guide_keywords Character vector asset names count reviewer guide presence. weights Named list relative indicator weights summing 1: label_quality, define_completeness, annotation_coverage, reviewer_guide.

title: Default Usability Assessment Configuration — usability_config_default
code: cfg <- usability_config_default() cfg$label_min_chars #> [1] 3 cfg$weights$label_quality #> [1] 0.3

title: Assess All Usability Indicators — usability_indicators
text: Convenience wrapper runs four usability assessments returns combined evidence table. primary entry point r4subusability package.

title: Assess All Usability Indicators — usability_indicators
code: usability_indicators(metadata, assets = character(0), ctx, config = NULL)

title: Assess All Usability Indicators — usability_indicators
text: metadata data frame columns dataset, variable, label, origin, derivation (optional), codelist (optional). Compatible adam_metadata sdtm_metadata datasets r4subdata. assets character vector asset names present submission package. Used assess_reviewer_guide(). Defaults character(0). ctx run context created r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(). config configuration list usability_config_default(). NULL default configuration used.

title: Assess All Usability Indicators — usability_indicators
text: validated evidence tibble combining results four usability assessments (see r4subcore::as_evidence()).

title: Assess All Usability Indicators — usability_indicators
code: # \donttest{ ctx <- r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY01") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112447-f7ayh65s" meta <- data.frame(   dataset    = c("ADSL", "ADSL", "ADAE"),   variable   = c("USUBJID", "AGE", "AETERM"),   label      = c("Unique Subject Identifier", "Age", "Adverse Event Term"),   origin     = c("CRF", "Derived", "CRF"),   derivation = c(NA, "Derived from BRTHDTC and RFSTDTC", NA),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) assets <- c("ADRG", "define.xml") ev <- usability_indicators(meta, assets = assets, ctx = ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 3 rows #> ✔ Evidence table created: 3 rows #> ✔ Evidence table created: 2 rows #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row #> ✔ Bound 4 evidence tables: 9 total rows nrow(ev) #> [1] 9 table(ev$result) #>  #>   na pass  #>    1    8  # }

title: Summarise Usability Evidence — usability_summary
text: Produces structured summary usability evidence, suitable printing passing downstream reporting functions.

title: Summarise Usability Evidence — usability_summary
code: usability_summary(evidence)

title: Summarise Usability Evidence — usability_summary
text: evidence validated evidence tibble usability_indicators() individual assessment functions.

title: Summarise Usability Evidence — usability_summary
text: usability_result list (class "usability_result") : study_id study identifier evidence. n_vars Number unique variable locations assessed. summary tibble per-indicator pass/fail/warn counts pct_pass (proportion passing rows). evidence original evidence tibble.

title: Summarise Usability Evidence — usability_summary
code: ctx <- r4subcore::r4sub_run_context(study_id = "STUDY01") #> ℹ Run context created: "R4S-20260316112448-11kz8bwc" meta <- data.frame(   dataset  = c("ADSL", "ADSL"),   variable = c("USUBJID", "AGE"),   label    = c("Unique Subject Identifier", "Age"),   origin   = c("CRF", "Derived"),   derivation = c(NA, "Derived from BRTHDTC"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ev  <- usability_indicators(meta, ctx = ctx) #> ✔ Evidence table created: 2 rows #> ✔ Evidence table created: 2 rows #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row #> ✔ Evidence table created: 1 row #> ✔ Bound 4 evidence tables: 6 total rows res <- usability_summary(ev) print(res) #>  #> ── R4SUB Usability Assessment ── #>  #> Study: "STUDY01" | Variables assessed: 4 #>  #> ✓ Variable Label Quality: 100% pass (2/2) #> ✓ Define-XML Completeness: 100% pass (2/2) #> ✓ Annotation Coverage: 100% pass (1/1) #> ✗ Reviewer Guide Presence: 0% pass (0/1) #>  #> ℹ Overall usability score: 75%

title: r4subusability 0.1.0
text: Initial release. usability_config_default(): default configuration usability assessments. assess_label_quality(): check variable label presence length (U-001). assess_define_completeness(): check Define-XML origin derivation fields (U-002). assess_annotation_coverage(): check proportion derived variables annotated (U-003). assess_reviewer_guide(): check reviewer guide presence submission package (U-004). usability_indicators(): run four assessments one call. usability_summary() print.usability_result(): summarise display results.
